mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases household income and consumption capacity. This drives equity valuations higher through expected earnings growth over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159561 thousand as of September 2013
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with steady employment gains
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% are not significantly negative compared to periods when FEDFUNDS <= 4.0% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the multiplier effect on corporate earnings drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand momentum that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1361 thousand and RSAFS at 724131 million as of August 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive months in HOUST and RSAFS do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than periods with divergent signals (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 103.5 index points signals manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion. This indicates firms are investing in capital and production, supporting earnings growth and driving positive 5-day equity returns as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with INDPRO > 103.5 do not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than months with INDPRO <= 103.5 (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power. This triggers a negative feedback loop where lower consumption reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth downward.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods when UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly negative compared to periods when UNRATE <= 4.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate move jointly to signal real monetary policy stance. When CPIAUCSL growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, the central bank is effectively easing in real terms, supporting equity valuations through lower real discount rates over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 320.58 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of September 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered periods do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly signals aggregate demand strength. When GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, the economy is expanding above trend, supporting corporate earnings and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained growth momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23528.047 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% do not produce significantly higher subsequent 60-day returns than quarters with growth <= 2.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline below 103.0 combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.3%) signals simultaneous supply contraction and labor market weakness. This dual deterioration reduces both productive capacity and aggregate demand, producing negative 20-day equity returns as the market reprices recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.88 and UNRATE at 4.2% as of August-September 2013
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with labor market stress
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered periods do not produce significantly negative 20-day returns compared to non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).
